entries.title: Dot Product
entries.content: Combines two vectors into a scalar. Geometrically: |a||b|cos θ. Zero dot product means vectors are perpendicular (orthogonal).

entries.title: Matrix Multiplication
entries.content: Product AB defined when columns of A equal rows of B. Entry (i,j) is dot product of row i of A and column j of B. Not commutative: AB ≠ BA in general.

entries.title: Determinant (2×2)
entries.content: Scalar value indicating if a matrix is invertible. Zero determinant means singular (no inverse). For 2×2, ad - bc.

entries.title: Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors
entries.content: For matrix A, vector v is an eigenvector if Av = λv for scalar λ (eigenvalue). Used in PCA, stability analysis, and quantum mechanics.

entries.title: Vector Magnitude
entries.content: Length of a vector in Euclidean space. Generalizes Pythagorean theorem to n dimensions.
